documents.text: List of longest-serving Indian chief ministers - Wikipedia The longest-serving chief minister of the Republic of India is former chief minister of Sikkim Pawan Kumar Chamling of the Sikkim Democratic Front, who served ...

List of Longest Serving Chief Ministers in India Who is the Chief Minister? The Governor appoints the Chief Minister. The Chief Minister is the head of the state. According to Article 163 of the Indian Constitution, the Council of Ministers is headed by the Chief Minister. The Chief Minister’s Council of Ministers consists of Cabinet Ministers, Ministers of State and Deputy Ministers. The Chief Minister resigns only when most of the Legislative Assembly members turn against him. The Chief Minister is the chief spokesperson of the state. Here we will know who is the longest-serving Chief Ministers in India. How many years is the tenure of the Chief Minister? The post of Chief Minister in the state is equal to that of the Prime Minister of the country. All bills and taxes can be imposed only after the approval of the Chief Minister. The Chief Minister of any state needs the support of the members of the Legislative Assembly. The tenure of the Chief Minister is 5 years. List of Longest Serving Chief Ministers in India The tenure of the Chief Minister is 5 years, but many Chief Ministers have been Chief Ministers one or more times or have come to power repeatedly.

[Solved] Who among the following is the longest serving Chief Ministe Pawan Kumar Chamling served as the Chief Minister of Sikkim from December 12, 1994, to May 27, 2019, making him the longest-serving Chief Minister in India. Detailed Solution Pawan Kumar Chamling served as the Chief Minister of Sikkim from December 12, 1994, to May 27, 2019, making him the longest-serving Chief Minister in India. His tenure lasted for over 24 years. He is the founder of the Sikkim Democratic Front (SDF). Under his leadership, Sikkim saw significant development in various sectors including education, healthcare, and infrastructure. Additional InformationLal Thanhawla: Served as the Chief Minister of Mizoram for multiple terms. Gegong Apang: Served as the Chief Minister of Arunachal Pradesh for a total of 22 years. Jyoti Basu: Served as the Chief Minister of West Bengal from 1977 to 2000, making him one of the longest-serving Chief Ministers, but his tenure was shorter than Chamling’s. Important PointsPadmaja Naidu served as the Governor of West Bengal for the longest period in Indian history. She held the office from 1956 to 1967, serving for 11 years. Dr. Rajendra Prasad is the longest-serving President of India. He served two terms from 1950 to 1962, totaling 12 years. He was the first President of India. Jawaharlal Nehru is the longest-serving Prime Minister of India. He served from August 15, 1947, to May 27, 1964, for a total of 16 years and 286 days.

Longest serving Chief Minister of any Indian State Chief Minister Pawan Chamling has been included in the World Book of Records for being the longest serving Chief Minister of any Indian State. Longest serving Chief Minister of any Indian State GANGTOK: Chief Minister Pawan Chamling has been included in the World Book of Records for being the longest serving Chief Minister of any Indian State by World Book of Records, London. The Chief Minister was bestowed with the Certificate of Record by the officials of World Book of Records here at Mintokgang, his official residence. The visiting delegates from World Book of Records including Dr. Diwakar Sukul, chairman World Book of Records, London, Shri Kusumakar Sukul from Delhi, Shri Santosh Shukla, president, World Book of Records, Mumbai and Shri Vikram Trivedi, advocate in the High Court of Madhya Pradesh and educationist called on the Chief Minister and bestowed him with the honour. The officials of the World Book of Records were accompanied by educationists from Sikkim and few north eastern States. On this occasion, Shri Deepak Adhikari, Shri Sanjoy Dolo and others were present. The Chief Minister expressed his gratitude to the officials from the World Book of Records for the recognition and for their gesture. He shared that he is always driven by an innate passion to serve the people of Sikkim. The visiting delegates lauded the unique feat achieved by the Chief Minister and expressed great joy in meeting him personally and presenting the honour. World Book of Records, London chairman Dr. Diwakar Sukul complimented the Chief Minister for his record tenure as the Chief Minister of an Indian State and for the relentless service to the people of Sikkim and the country. He said the Chief Minister is one of the most popular leaders in the country who has had the continued love and support of the people. He also conveyed the congratulations and good wishes sent by Virendra Sharma, Member of British Parliament. Significantly, the World Book of Records is an England-based organization also having its association with Alma, India. Alma has been espousing the very cause of education and philanthropic activities across the nation and abroad since the last 18 years.

Pawan Kumar Chamling - Wikipedia He is the longest serving Chief Minister in India, surpassing Naveen Patnaik. Chamling is the Founding President of the Sikkim Democratic Front. Pawan Kumar Chamling Rai (born 22 September 1950[1]) is an Indian politician and writer who served as the 5th Chief Minister of Sikkim from 1994 to 2019. He is the longest serving Chief Minister in India, surpassing Naveen Patnaik.[2][3] Chamling is the Founding President of the Sikkim Democratic Front.[4][5] He also served as the Leader of the opposition, Sikkim Legislative Assembly in the year of 2019. He represents the Namchi-Singhithang constituency in the Sikkim Legislative Assembly since 2019 till 2024 and the Damthang constituency from 1985 to 1994. Prior to establishing the Sikkim Democratic Front, Chamling served as Minister for Industries, Information and Public Relations from 1989 to 1992 in Nar Bahadur Bhandari's cabinet. Personal life Chamling was born in Yangang, South Sikkim to Nepali parents Ash Bahadur Chamling Rai and Asha Rani Chamling Rai. Chamling is also a prolific Nepali language writer, and recipient of the Bhanu Puraskar (2010) awarded by Sikkim Sahitya Parishad. He writes under the pen name Pawan Chamling. Mr. Chamling has two wives and 8 children (4 sons and 4 daughters).[6][7][8] Political career Chamling was elected as the president of Yangang Gram Panchayat in 1982. In 1985, he was elected to the Sikkim Legislative Assembly for the first time. After being elected for the second time from Damthang, he became the Minister for Industries, Information and Public Relations from 1989 to 1992 in the Nar Bahadur Bhandari cabinet. After a series of major political upheavals in Sikkim, Chamling formed the Sikkim Democratic Front on 4 March 1993.

5 Longest Serving Chief Ministers of India Pawan Kumar Chamling tops the list of the longest-serving chief minister with a tenure of 24 years and 165 days. News & Events 5 Longest Serving Chief Ministers of India India is a big country where governance happens at various levels, from local panchayat and municipal corporations to state and central government. State government is one of the crucial layers of governance that take care of the administration and development of the state. The head of the state is the Chief Minister, who is assisted by a council of ministers. A chief minister makes crucial decisions for the state in various aspects and benefits the people of the state. Over the years, several chief ministers of India have left an incredible mark on the hearts of people, which is evident from their work and long tenure. Here are the top 5 longest-serving chief ministers of India. 1. Pawan Kumar Chamling Pawan Kumar Chamling tops the list of the longest-serving chief minister with a tenure of 24 years and 165 days. He remains the chief minister of the beautiful northeastern state of India, “Sikkim” from 1994 to 2019. He established the Sikkim Democratic Front, a regional political party under which he governed the state for five consecutive terms. Before becoming the chief minister of the state, he served as the Minister of Information, Information and Public Relations from 1989 to 1992 in the cabinet of Nar Bahudar Bhandari, who was the chief minister of Sikkim from 1984 to 1985. Work and achievements Pawan Kumar Chamling played a vital role in transforming Sikkim into an organic state. He took several bold decisions to promote organic farming in the hill state. The starting point was the withdrawal of the chemical fertilizers and pesticides. Then rigid steps were taken, which included making laws for completely banning the use of chemical fertilizers and pesticides. Other policies that were set in motion were “One Village”, “One Crop”, and funding programmes to train farmers and make infrastructure for organic farming.

Who is the Longest Serving Chief Minister of Delhi, Check Here! Sheila Dikshit holds the record for the longest-serving chief minister of New Delhi with a total tenure of 15 years and 25 days. Who is the Longest Serving Chief Minister of Delhi, Check Here! First Female Chief Minister of New Delhi: Sheila Dikshit holds the record for the longest-serving chief minister of New Delhi with a total tenure of 15 years and 25 days. She was elected as the CM of New Delhi in the year 1998 and served three consecutive terms until 2013. During her time in office, she focused on improving infrastructure, healthcare, and education in the city. Since its establishment, Delhi has had a total of eight Chief Ministers. The first was Chaudhary Brahm Prakash, who served from 1952 to 1955. Over the years, Delhi has seen several leaders take charge, each contributing to the city's growth. The longest-serving Chief Minister of Delhi is Sheila Dikshit. She held office for over 15 years, from 1998 to 2013. Sheila Dikshit played a crucial role in modernising Delhi. Her tenure saw major infrastructure developments, better public transport, and improved governance. Under her leadership, the Delhi Metro expanded, roads improved, and public services became more efficient. In this article, we will take a closer look at the history of Delhi’s Chief Ministers. We will explore their contributions and understand how leadership has shaped the capital.
